intended_use: SomnoMetry is intended for use for the diagnostic evaluation by a physician to assess sleep quality and as an aid for the diagnosis of sleep and respiratory-related sleep disorders in adults only. Som
intended_use_full: SomnoMetry is intended for use for the diagnostic evaluation by a physician to assess sleep quality and as an aid for the diagnosis of sleep and respiratory-related sleep disorders in adults only. SomnoMetry is a software-only medical device to be used to analyze physiological signals and automatically score sleep study results, including the staging of sleep, AHI, and detection of sleep-disordered breathing events including obstructive apneas. It is intended to be used under the supervision of a clinician in a clinical environment. All automatically scored events are subject to verification by a qualified clinician.
target_condition: Sleep and respiratory-related sleep disorders, including obstructive sleep apnea
ground_truth: AASM gold standard of manually scored PSG data from AASM-accredited Sleep Testing Facilities
other_metrics: For sleep staging (Endpoint 1): Positive Agreement (PA) 90.6% (AHI≥5), 83.3% (AHI≥15), 80.0% (AHI≥30); Negative Agreement (NA) 92.2% (AHI≥5), 97.5% (AHI≥15), 94.7% (AHI≥30); Overall Agreement (OA) 91.2% (AHI≥5), 95.6% (AHI≥15), 88.9% (AHI≥30). For REM sleep: PA 89.1% (AHI≥5), 85.6% (AHI≥15), 78.8% (AHI≥30); NA 94.9% (AHI≥5), 94.7% (AHI≥15), 95.6% (AHI≥30); OA 92.8% (AHI≥5), 88.9% (AHI≥15), 92.4% (AHI≥30). Confusion matrix provided showing stage-by-stage classification performance. Subgroup analyses by age (<65 and ≥65) demonstrated generalizability. Likelihood ratios reported across thresholds.
